Article Title: 2 Congress MLAs to join Vijay govt, no AIADMK rebels for now
Article Text:
.





CHENNAI: Tamil Nadu chief minister C Joseph Vijay will expand his cabinet to induct two Congress legislators as ministers on Thursday, reports Julie Mariappan.Members of the AIADMK faction headed by former ministers C Ve Shanmugam and S P Velumani are, however, not to be included in this round of cabinet expansion, sources told TOI. Congress floor leader K Rajesh Kumar and P Viswanathan are expected to be sworn in at 10am at Lok Bhavan.R V Arlekar, who holds the additional charge of TN governor, is expected to arrive in Chennai on Wednesday night. "We will be part of the Tamil Nadu cabinet after six decades. A long dream of our cadres is coming true," a Congress neta said. Vijay has faced criticism for leaving nearly 20 departments without ministers, including agriculture and higher education.
Article Summary: Tamil Nadu chief minister C Joseph Vijay will expand his cabinet to induct two Congress legislators as ministers on Thursday. Congress floor leader K Rajesh Kumar and P Viswanathan are expected to be sworn in at 10am at Lok Bhavan. Vijay has faced criticism for leaving nearly 20 departments without ministers, including agriculture and higher education.

Article Title: BJP: Congress silence on Vijay's 'Eelam genocide' brazen opportunism
Article Text:
.





NEW DELHI/CHENNAI: Tamil Nadu CM C Joseph Vijay's statement expressing "solidarity for the rights of our Tamil kin living across the seas" on the anniversary of the 2009 military offensive against LTTE in Sri Lanka's Mullivaikkal led BJP to question Tuesday whether this didn't touch a raw nerve in Congress "This is unacceptable. LTTE not only masterminded the killing of our former PM Rajiv Gandhi but was also responsible for the deaths of hundreds of brave jawans of the Indian army who had, as part of the Indian Peace Keeping Force, been sent to help restore peace. Yet Congress decides to keep mum. This is brazen political expediency," former I&B minister Anurag Thakur said.BJP's Amit Malviya said: "Of course, Rahul Gandhi would have no problem with it, as long as Congress gets a slice of power. After all, DMK too was an LTTE apologist." In his first statement on Eelam Tamils since becoming CM, Vijay avoided naming LTTE, its slain chief Velupillai Prabhakaran, or even Sri Lanka. Congress is yet to respond to Vijay's statement.
Article Summary: Tamil Nadu CM C Joseph Vijay's statement expresses "solidarity for the rights of our Tamil kin living across the seas" Vijay avoided naming LTTE, its slain chief Velupillai Prabhakaran, or even Sri Lanka.
